title: China-to-South Africa Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline Versus South Africa Approval Evidence
rows.target_requirement: South Africa market deployment combines product-scope checks, safety evidence, EMC evidence, and project-specific grid-connection approval. The target file may therefore need NRCS LOA evidence, SANS / IEC safety and EMC reports, NRS 097-2-1 test evidence, Eskom or municipal acceptance, and local labels or instructions.
target_standards: NRCS LOA route where compulsory specifications apply
SANS / CISPR / IEC EMC evidence where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: A common China baseline for PV inverters includes GB/T 37408 for grid-connected inverter technical requirements, GB/T 19964 for PV power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004 for PV grid-connected inverter technical specification, GB EMC reports, CQC voluntary certificates, and IEC CB certificates.
rows.gap: China baseline evidence is useful for engineering due diligence but must be translated into South Africa's legal and project evidence structure. The exporter should build a crosswalk that maps each China report to the South African requirement it supports and identifies missing LOA, NRS 097, SANS edition, EMC, label, importer, or commissioning items.

mandatory_status.target: Mixed — LOA, grid connection, safety, and EMC evidence become mandatory only where the applicable South African regulatory, distributor, customer, insurer, or project route requires them.
mandatory_status.cn: Mixed — GB/T and CQC evidence are often recommended or market-driven for domestic and export files; project grid requirements may make specific evidence effectively required in China.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China GB/T baseline is a starting evidence pack, not a South Africa approval pack. Build a requirement-by-requirement crosswalk before quoting compliance or shipping.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / National Public Service Platform for Standards Information

rows.requirement: EMC Evidence Under SANS Adoptions of CISPR / IEC Standards
rows.target_requirement: PV inverters use high-frequency power electronics, so South African technical files may require EMC evidence under SANS adoptions of CISPR and IEC EMC standards, such as radio-disturbance, harmonics, flicker, immunity, and installation-specific compatibility evidence. The applicable standard set depends on the product classification, connection point, and approval or project route.
target_standards: SANS adoption of CISPR radio-disturbance standards where applicable
SANS adoption of IEC 61000 EMC standards where applicable
Project or distributor power-quality and EMC evidence requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China EMC files may reference GB 4824 / CISPR 11 alignment, GB 17625 harmonic-current limits, GB/T 17626 immunity tests, or other GB EMC standards. These reports can support a technical comparison but should be checked against the exact SANS / CISPR / IEC edition and South African acceptance route.
rows.gap: The gap is edition mapping, laboratory acceptability, and installation context. Exporters should align conducted and radiated emissions, harmonic current, flicker, immunity, cable lengths, filters, and earthing assumptions with the South African file rather than relying only on a China EMC report.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by NRCS, SANS conformity route, project owner, insurer, distributor, or installation approval; exact EMC standard selection is product- and project-specific.
mandatory_status.cn: Not directly equivalent — China GB EMC reports need edition and acceptance mapping before use in South Africa.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EMC evidence should be mapped to the accepted SANS / CISPR / IEC route for the specific inverter and project; a China GB EMC report is helpful evidence, not automatic South African acceptance.

rows.target_requirement: South African distribution-grid projects use a 50 Hz nominal frequency, but inverter acceptance depends on the NRS 097-2-1 setting profile and the distributor's approved connection file rather than the nominal frequency alone. Firmware profiles, frequency trip limits, reconnection delays, and active-power response should be locked or documented for the South African profile before commissioning.
target_standards: Eskom or municipal inverter settings and commissioning records
South Africa 50 Hz nominal distribution-grid profile
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses 50 Hz grids and Chinese PV inverter files may include GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, or NB/T 32004 frequency tests. That technical overlap does not prove that the South African NRS 097 profile, trip windows, reconnection behavior, or distributor-specific settings are loaded.
rows.gap: Do not treat 50 Hz compatibility as complete grid compliance. The evidence package should show the exact South Africa firmware profile, parameter-locking method, installer access controls, and commissioning screenshots or records requested by the distributor.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as part of grid connection where the distributor requires NRS 097 settings and commissioning evidence; nominal 50 Hz rating alone is not an approval.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China 50 Hz test evidence does not confirm South African grid-profile settings.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] South Africa and China both use 50 Hz, but exporters still need NRS 097 / distributor-specific frequency settings and commissioning evidence for the actual project.

source.publisher: Small-scale Embedded Generation Municipal Resource Portal / SALGA

rows.requirement: NRS 097-2-1 Utility Interface for Embedded Generation
rows.target_requirement: South African embedded-generation projects commonly use NRS 097-2-1 for the utility interface of embedded generation, and the SSEG municipal resource portal lists NRS097-2-1:2024 as the current grid-connection utility-interface document. Eskom and municipalities may require inverter certificates, approved-inverter-list evidence, settings sheets, single-line diagrams, commissioning records, and project-specific acceptance before parallel operation.
target_standards: NRS097-2-1:2024 Grid Connection of Embedded Generation - Utility Interface
Eskom or municipal embedded-generation application and commissioning requirements
City or distributor approved inverter list where used
rows.china_equivalent: China grid evidence under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, State Grid, or China Southern Power Grid procedures can support engineering review but does not transfer as NRS 097-2-1 conformity or Eskom / municipal permission to connect.
rows.gap: The main gap is grid-interface behavior and local project acceptance: anti-islanding, voltage and frequency trip settings, reconnection timing, power quality, protection coordination, and commissioning records must match the NRS 097 route and the relevant distributor's file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for projects that connect embedded generation in parallel with the South African distribution network where the relevant Eskom or municipal distributor requires NRS 097-2-1 evidence.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China grid-code evidence does not authorize South African grid connection.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat NRS 097-2-1 and the Eskom or municipal connection file as a project approval gate separate from NRCS product approval and IEC 62109 safety evidence.

source.publisher: Small-scale Embedded Generation Municipal Resource Portal / SALGA

rows.requirement: NRCS Letter of Authority (LOA) and VC 8055 Scope Check
rows.target_requirement: South Africa's NRCS electrotechnical regulator administers Letters of Authority for electrical and electronic apparatus that fall within compulsory specifications such as VC 8055. A PV inverter exporter should treat LOA as a scope-confirmation and model-approval gate where the product is covered, but should not assume that every inverter configuration is automatically mandatory without checking the current NRCS compulsory-specification scope and application route.
target_standards: NRCS electrotechnical approvals and Letter of Authority process
VC 8055 compulsory specification for electrical and electronic apparatus, where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic files for PV inverters commonly include GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004, CQC voluntary certificates, CB reports, or factory quality records. These can support the South Africa technical file but do not replace an NRCS LOA where the South African compulsory-specification route applies.
rows.gap: The gap is regulatory scope and local approval evidence, not only engineering performance. Prepare model identification, ratings, test reports, user instructions, labels, importer details, and any SANS / IEC evidence needed for the NRCS file before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the inverter model is within the applicable NRCS compulsory-specification scope; scope and LOA route must be confirmed with NRCS or a qualified South African conformity professional.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China GB/T, CQC, or CB evidence does not by itself create a South Africa NRCS LOA.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not ship on the assumption that a China or CB inverter file is enough. First confirm NRCS / VC 8055 scope and obtain an LOA where the compulsory-specification route applies.

rows.requirement: SANS / IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: PV inverter safety evidence for South Africa is commonly built around the IEC 62109 family for power converters used in photovoltaic systems, including general converter safety and inverter-specific requirements. Exporters should confirm the accepted SANS adoption, edition, national deviations, and test-report route for the NRCS, customer, insurer, or project file before relying on an existing CB certificate.
target_standards: SANS adoption of IEC 62109-1, as accepted for the project or approval route
SANS adoption of IEC 62109-2, as accepted for the project or approval route
IEC 62109 series for PV power converter safety
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese manufacturers may already hold CB Scheme reports to IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, CQC voluntary certificates, or GB/T-based inverter reports. Those files can reduce technical duplication, but acceptance depends on the South African route and does not by itself prove NRCS LOA, NRS grid approval, or municipal project acceptance.
rows.gap: The key documentation gap is mapping the exact model, firmware, ratings, isolation topology, protective devices, and labels in the China or CB file to the South African model being supplied. Any national deviations, plug or wiring assumptions, and installation instructions should be checked for South Africa.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by NRCS LOA scope, project owner, insurer, installer, or grid-connection file; IEC / SANS evidence is usually a technical evidence route, not a standalone market approval by itself.
mandatory_status.cn: Often voluntary or market-driven for export files; not a substitute for South African acceptance.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is central to the safety file, but exporters should confirm the accepted SANS edition and local approval route before treating an existing China or CB report as sufficient.
